title: OntologyReindexRecord
description: Wire format of one record published to the Kafka topic `ontology.reindex.v1`. Producer: services/reindex-coordinator-service (FASE 4 / Tarea 4.2 — Rust replacement for the legacy `workers-go/reindex` Temporal worker, removed in Tarea 4.3). Consumer: services/ontology-indexer, which materialises the record into the search backend (Vespa prod / OpenSearch dev). The shape MUST stay aligned with `ontology.object.changed.v1` so the indexer decodes both topics with the same code path (see services/ontology-indexer/src/lib.rs::ObjectChangedV1).

tenant.description: Tenant identifier the record belongs to. Used as the first half of the Kafka partition key (`<tenant>/<id>`).

id.description: Stable object identifier (UUID string in production, but typed as opaque string for forward compatibility).

type_id.description: Ontology type identifier of the object.

version.description: Monotonically increasing per-object revision number. Backends discard writes older than the currently indexed version (ADR-0028).

payload.description: Full materialised object payload to index. Backends decide what to expose via their schema.

embedding.description: Optional dense vector pass-through. The producer forwards whatever was in `objects_by_id.properties.embedding` verbatim; the indexer writes it to the registered `embedding` field when present.

deleted.description: Tombstone flag. The reindex producer always emits `false` (deleted rows are filtered out before publish), but the field is accepted for shape parity with `ontology.object.changed.v1`.
